untranslatable_reason: Signal requires activity-subordinated bar construction (dynamic bar formation based on cumulative volume thresholds) and Jarque-Bera statistical test for return normality. Neither dynamic bar resampling logic nor parametric normality testing (Jarque-Bera p-value) are available in the allowed indicator set. The core mechanism of replacing fixed time intervals with volume-based bar formation cannot be expressed using point-in-time indicator comparisons alone.
translation_notes: Attempted proxy: Used cumulative volume threshold (volume >= threshold) for entry and hvol_20 heteroscedasticity detection for exit rebalancing. However, this does not capture the essential mechanism of the signal: dynamic bar construction subordinated to market activity rather than fixed time intervals. Jarque-Bera normality testing would require access to distributional statistics not available in the indicator library. The signal fundamentally requires a different backtesting engine architecture than condition-based rule matching.
